eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro at 64.3% to remain Venezuela's head of state by December 31, 2026, reflecting uncertainty over official recognition criteria like UN listings and government directories, even after his January 3 U.S. capture and transfer to New York custody on drug and weapons charges. Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as interim president on January 5, holds 22.5% as she consolidates control through recent purges of Maduro allies—including arrests of loyalists—and appointments of security loyalists, boosting Venezuelan oil exports amid U.S. engagement under President Trump. Opposition leader María Corina Machado's 8% odds stem from her April unification of anti-regime forces and plans to return home by year-end, though no election date is set despite constitutional pressures for a vote within months of presidential absence, leaving transition prospects unclear.

markets.question: Atlanta Braves vs. Colorado Rockies: O/U 15.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Atlanta Braves and Colorado Rockies, scheduled for May 3 at 3:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Atlanta Braves and Colorado Rockies combine to score 16 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 16, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Atlanta Braves vs. Colorado Rockies
events.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Atlanta Braves and Colorado Rockies, scheduled for May 3 at 3:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Atlanta Braves" if the Atlanta Braves win the game.

This market will resolve to "Colorado Rockies" if the Colorado Rockies win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlanta Braves ace Spencer Strider makes his season debut following an oblique strain, facing Colorado Rockies lefty Kyle Freeland in Sunday's series finale at hitter-friendly Coors Field, where the Braves hold a 2-0 edge after blowout wins including 9-1 Saturday. Atlanta's NL East-leading 24-10 record and 5.7 runs per game reflect hot recent form, contrasting the Rockies' NL West-bottoming 14-20 mark and pitching woes. Primary uncertainty: Ronald Acuña Jr.'s status after left hamstring tightness forced his early exit yesterday, with MRI results pending; he's day-to-day per official injury report. High-altitude conditions and bullpen depth, bolstered by recent Braves call-ups, further influence trader sentiment on this matchup.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 45% implied probability that the peak US unemployment rate (U-3 measure) reaches 5.0% or higher in any 2026 month, balancing recent labor market resilience against forecasts of modest softening. The March 2026 BLS report showed unemployment ticking down to 4.3% with 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains—reversing February's weakness—while jobless claims remain elevated. FOMC March projections hold median Q4 2026 unemployment at 4.4%, matching Philadelphia Fed Survey at 4.5%, though some economists eye 4.6-4.8% amid cooling hiring. April FOMC noted low but stable job growth; traders await the imminent April employment report and June FOMC for signals on recession risks and Fed funds path.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 140-159 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Trump’s approval rating be between 39.0 and 39.4 on May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Silver Bulletin's approval rating for Donald Trump on May 8, 2026.

Note that the approval ratings for this date must be finalized before it is considered for this market (namely, once the next data point is available, the previous one is finalized).

This market's resolution source will be Silver Bulletin's approval rating poll aggregator, https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-ratings-nate-silver-bulletin, specifically the approval rating indicated by the green trend line for the resolution date. Changes in the methodology by which Silver Bulletin calculates the approval rating will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. If Silver Bulletin's approval rating becomes permanently unavailable, RealClearPolitics will be used.

The resolution source reports the rating value to only one decimal point (e.g., 42.8%, 33.9%, etc.). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid leads trader consensus for the Hart Memorial Trophy at 50% implied probability after dominating the 2025-26 regular season with 138 points to secure his sixth Art Ross Trophy, surging ahead in the final weeks with 90 assists despite Edmonton missing playoffs. Nikita Kucherov sits second at 25% following a strong 130-point campaign for Tampa Bay, earning early Hart favoritism in media polls like the NHL Trophy Tracker with 10 first-place votes. Nathan MacKinnon's 19% reflects his 127 points and Maurice "Rocket" Richard Trophy win with 53 goals for Colorado, though voters prioritize overall impact. Macklin Celebrini's 3.6% highlights rookie promise amid San Jose's rebuild. With Hart finalists announced May 8, markets reflect regular-season stat leaders amid high-volume trading.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be between 54-55°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the San Francisco International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a 62-63°F high temperature (50% implied probability) at San Francisco International Airport (KSFO) for May 4, aligned with the latest National Weather Service forecast showing a high near 63°F under partly sunny skies with west winds around 10 mph. This positioning stems from a deepened marine layer—stratus clouds and cool, moist onshore flow from the Pacific—enhanced by an offshore upper low tracking south, capping coastal highs as seen in recent model consensus from GFS and ECMWF runs updated this morning. Following below-normal temperatures earlier this week, including 58°F on May 1, the pattern persists amid cool sea surface temperatures around 55°F. A partial afternoon burn-off could nudge toward 64-65°F (29.5%), but persistent cloud cover limits upside; KSFO hourly observations will resolve the market tomorrow.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the third-most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

Recent Sagalytics polling (April 23-29) shows AD+PD holding steady at 3.7%, outpacing Momentum's volatile 2-11% range despite the latter's recent full-slate candidate announcements, clean financial filings, and anti-corruption proposals. AD+PD's established green platform and non-coalition cooperation pact with Momentum bolster trader consensus at 65% implied probability, while fragmented "others" like Imperium Europa dilute challengers in Malta's single transferable vote system. Short campaign timeline heightens risks from undecideds and turnout.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held its federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% after the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, with Chair Powell affirming the policy's suitability amid resurgent inflation. March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while core PCE hit 3.2%, underscoring sticky price pressures despite solid labor data: +178,000 nonfarm payrolls and steady 4.3% unemployment. CME FedWatch Tool reflects trader consensus with roughly 45% implied probability of a 2027 rate hike, pricing funds toward 3.79% by late that year. Traders eye April CPI (May 12 release), upcoming nonfarm payrolls, and the June 16-17 FOMC for signals on monetary policy trajectory before 2027.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following its May 1 wide theatrical release by 20th Century Studios, The Devil Wears Prada 2 has earned a 77% Tomatometer score on Rotten Tomatoes from 224 critic reviews, surpassing the original 2006 film's 75% and achieving Certified Fresh status in early tallies before slight adjustments. Traders note strong praise for Meryl Streep's iconic Miranda Priestly, Anne Hathaway's matured Andy Sachs, Emily Blunt's scene-stealing Emily, and Stanley Tucci's heartfelt Nigel, alongside timely satire on media consolidation and digital disruption in fashion publishing. Criticisms center on familiar beats lacking the debut's razor-sharp bite and occasional momentum lulls. With an 88% audience score, A- CinemaScore, and robust opening weekend tracking, the score may shift as more reviews pour in over the next few days, a common post-premiere dynamic for review aggregators.

markets.question: Will Bernie Sanders say "ICE" 3+ times in Michigan?
markets.description:
Bernie Sanders is scheduled to speak in Detroit, Michigan on his Fighting Oligarchy tour on May 3, 2026 (https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/rsvp-oligarchy-detroit/?source=website-oligarchy). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bernie Sanders says the listed term during events in Detroit scheduled for May 3, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Sanders is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Bernie Sanders's remarks in Detroit scheduled for May 3, 2026. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Kostyantynivka by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.528896° N, 37.702514° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Kostyantynivka will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/p9N3w6yRVfjx6cuZ8

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces reached the outskirts of Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast within the past 48 hours, employing infiltration tactics to establish footholds amid heavy artillery and drone exchanges, while Ukrainian defenders repelled assaults using FPV drones and airstrikes on Russian positions. Over the prior month, incremental Russian advances in the Kostyantynivka-Druhzivka tactical area have stalled against Ukrainian resistance, with no confirmed seizure of the town center despite ongoing assaults near Dovha Balka and Pleshchiivka. Trader consensus reflects grinding attrition warfare, vulnerable to reinforcements, weather, or escalation in Pokrovsk sector clashes; monitor daily geolocated footage for shifts before the market's resolution date.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against another U.S. sovereign debt downgrade before 2027, reflecting stable outlooks from major agencies—S&P at AA+ stable, Fitch at AA+ stable, and Moody's at Aa1 stable—following Moody's May 2025 cut from Aaa amid fiscal deterioration. Despite Fitch's April 30, 2026, warning of deficits averaging 7.9% of GDP through 2028 and gross debt projected to hit 122% of GDP by end-2027, with public debt now exceeding GDP for the first time since World War II, no further rating actions have materialized, buoyed by the dollar's reserve currency status and institutional inertia. Key swing factors include debt ceiling negotiations and midterm budget battles later in 2026, which could test governance but have yet to shift agency views.

markets.question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification: Alina Korneeva vs Ella Seidel
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Alina Korneeva and Ella Seidel in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification, originally scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Alina Korneeva' if Alina Korneeva advances against Ella Seidel.

This market will resolve to 'Ella Seidel' if Ella Seidel advances against Alina Korneeva.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Recent drivers include Musk's wealth dipping to around $800 billion after a $35 billion drop amid Tesla stock volatility and broader market pressures, despite February's SpaceX-xAI merger valuing the entity at $1.25 trillion. No credible reports of a new pregnancy or child have emerged four months in, while Starship progress lags with Flight 12 now targeting late May after delays. Upcoming catalysts like successful launches and Tesla robotaxi/Optimus updates could shift sentiment, but historical timelines and execution risks sustain bearish positioning.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Elevated capex for AI data centers has sparked cash flow concerns, yet resilient e-commerce and record 13.1% operating margins support sentiment. Analyst consensus targets $308 (range $207–$370), implying modest upside by month-end, with Q2 results eyed for July 30 as the pivotal near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Opendoor is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Opendoor's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.10 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Opendoor reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.10 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Opendoor releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 18°C or below on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory (HKO) observations confirm the minimum air temperature reached 24°C at the reference station on May 2, 2026, aligning with the 99.7% market-implied probability and driving near-unanimous trader consensus backed by real capital. This matches HKO's pre-event 9-day forecast projecting 24°C minima under mainly cloudy skies with moderate humidity, consistent with May climatological norms where average daily lows hover around 24°C amid subtropical warming trends and normal to above-normal seasonal temperatures. Model consensus from HKO's multi-model system showed no significant deviations, reinforcing the outcome. Realistic challenges are minimal—only an unprecedented data revision from quality assurance audits could shift it, though HKO's measurement protocols ensure high reliability.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 100-119 Truth Social posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Viktor Orbán's commanding 97.2% implied probability stems from his confirmed electoral defeat in Hungary's April 12, 2026, parliamentary election, where opposition leader Péter Magyar's Tisza party secured a landslide two-thirds majority amid record 78% turnout, ending Orbán's 16-year tenure as prime minister. Orbán promptly conceded, relinquished his parliamentary seat, and shifted Fidesz to opposition, solidifying his exit from power well before 2027. Traders' near-unanimous consensus reflects this resolved outcome, with no credible challenges emerging from recounts or legal disputes. Only an unprecedented reversal—such as a successful constitutional court ruling invalidating results—could realistically disrupt this, though institutional acceptance and procedural finality render it improbable.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will Khamzat Chimaev be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

events.title: Who will be the next UFC Pound-For-Pound #1 in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Ilia Topuria as the slight favorite at 49% implied probability to claim UFC pound-for-pound #1 in 2026, buoyed by his rapid rise to #2 in official rankings after securing the lightweight title and his scheduled June matchup against top contender Justin Gaethje following an early-year hiatus. Islam Makhachev retains the top spot post-UFC 327, with his "Islam/None in 2026" outcome at 37% reflecting confidence in continued dominance amid rumors of a welterweight transition and August return versus Ian Garry. Alexandre Pantoja's 38.9% stems from elite grappling despite a title-losing arm injury to Joshua Van at UFC 323, eyeing a mid-year comeback; Petr Yan's 36% follows his bantamweight title reclamation over Merab Dvalishvili, though back surgery sidelines him until summer. Dricus du Plessis (29.3%) and Alex Pereira (24.1%) benefit from sustained middleweight and light heavyweight title reigns, respectively, in a fluid P4P landscape.

markets.question: Will Lee Jung-hyun win the 2026 Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Min Hyung-bae commands 98.2% trader consensus in the inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayoral race due to the region's entrenched status as a Honam stronghold, where his party routinely secures overwhelming majorities in local elections—often exceeding 70%—rooted in historical voting patterns and unified party support following his April 14 primary victory over rivals like Kim Young-rok. People Power Party's Lee Jung-hyun, nominated April 22 despite national experience as former party leader, faces formidable regional barriers, with conservatives historically under 30% there; his May 2 public debate proposal to Min has yet to shift dynamics. While late-breaking scandals, health issues, or a DPK campaign misstep could narrow the gap before the June 3 vote, structural partisan math sustains Min's dominance.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's dominant straight-sets win over Alexander Zverev in the Madrid Open final on May 1—capping titles at Indian Wells, Miami, and Monte-Carlo—has entrenched him as the 48% trader consensus favorite for the 2026 Men's Wimbledon, underscoring his world No. 1 form, 29-2 record, and improving grass-court potential after semifinals at the All England Club in 2023. Carlos Alcaraz trails at 17.5% implied probability, buoyed by consecutive Wimbledon triumphs and his Australian Open 2026 career Grand Slam completion, yet recent clay finals defeats to Sinner highlight head-to-head vulnerabilities. Novak Djokovic's persistent shoulder injury, prompting withdrawals from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid, has eroded his odds to 6.8% despite seven grass major titles, as traders weigh the 39-year-old's recovery against the fast surface demands.

markets.question: Will a U.S. anti-cartel operation outside of the United States occur by April 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participate on the ground in an anti-cartel operation or conduct a kinetic strike directed against a cartel on foreign soil by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count.

Only direct U.S. participation, confirmed by the U.S. Government or by an overwhelming consensus of reporting, will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government; however, an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Joint U.S.-Ecuadorian military operations launched on March 3, 2026, by U.S. Southern Command alongside Ecuadorian forces against designated narco-terrorist organizations like Los Lobos and Los Choneros represent the most significant recent escalation in U.S. anti-cartel efforts on foreign soil, though questions persist over whether U.S. personnel engaged in direct ground participation or kinetic strikes as opposed to support roles. The Trump administration's Americas Counter Cartel Coalition, bolstered by a 2025 presidential directive authorizing military action against Latin American cartels, has driven intensified collaborations, including a February U.S. task force aiding Mexico's hunt for Sinaloa leader El Mencho. An April 19 Chihuahua meth lab raid, where two CIA officers died in a post-operation crash, was officially described as advisory and drone surveillance by Mexican authorities, failing strict direct-action criteria. Traders monitor for official U.S. confirmations amid disputes, with June 30 deadlines and 2026 World Cup security potentially prompting further operations.

markets.question: Will San Antonio Spurs advance to the Conference Finals in the 2026 NBA Playoffs?
markets.description:

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to reach the Conference Finals of the 2026 NBA Playoffs (e.g. they are eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the results of the 2026 Playoffs Conference Semifinals have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA (https://www.nba.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: As NBA playoffs conference semifinals commence, trader consensus hinges on West matchups where top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder face injury-depleted Los Angeles Lakers after a 4-0 first-round sweep, and San Antonio Spurs meet Minnesota Timberwolves, with Anthony Edwards questionable (knee) following their 4-2 upset over Denver. In the East, Detroit Pistons—15th team ever to rally from 3-1 down—advanced past Orlando 4-3 (116-94 Game 7) and await Cleveland Cavaliers or Toronto Raptors Game 7 winner today, while New York Knicks host Philadelphia 76ers, who stunned Boston 4-3 on the road. Key factors include home-court edges, LeBron James' leadership amid Lakers' absences, and rest advantages for West series starters.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades near 1.17, reflecting persistent US-Eurozone monetary policy divergence as the Federal Reserve holds the fed funds target at 3.5%-3.75%—with notable internal dissent at the April 29 FOMC—while the ECB maintained its 2.0% deposit rate on April 30 amid reaccelerating inflation pressures. Eurozone April preliminary CPI hit 3.0% year-over-year, up from March's 2.6% on energy cost surges tied to Middle East tensions, as US March CPI jumped to 3.3%; both fuel trader caution on rate paths. Consensus analyst forecasts cluster around 1.18-1.19 mid-2026, but April US CPI due May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC loom as key catalysts for shifting rate differentials and pair momentum.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: New York's 13th Congressional District (D+32 Cook PVI) is a Democratic stronghold, with incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat securing 83% in the 2024 general election amid negligible Republican opposition. Current trader consensus pricing Democrats at 93.5% stems from the district's urban demographics in upper Manhattan and the Bronx, historical landslides, and a weak GOP primary field limited to Emmanuel Williams and Manual Williams. Recent focus has been the competitive June 23 Democratic primary, where DSA-endorsed challenger Darializa Avila Chevalier gained in an April internal poll (46%-35% over Espaillat post-messaging), but this does not threaten the party's general election dominance. Realistic challenges include a massive national Republican midterm wave or major Democratic nominee scandal.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held its federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% after the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, with Chair Powell affirming the policy's suitability amid resurgent inflation. March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while core PCE hit 3.2%, underscoring sticky price pressures despite solid labor data: +178,000 nonfarm payrolls and steady 4.3% unemployment. CME FedWatch Tool reflects trader consensus with roughly 45% implied probability of a 2027 rate hike, pricing funds toward 3.79% by late that year. Traders eye April CPI (May 12 release), upcoming nonfarm payrolls, and the June 16-17 FOMC for signals on monetary policy trajectory before 2027.

events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Chelsea FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.
